text.title: Framework Selection and Principle Audit
text.summary: IIBA Agile Extension seven-principle audit, Disciplined Agile lifecycle selection rules, framework comparison, and agent workflow for producing a framework-fit report.

rule.statement: Score each of the seven IIBA Agile Extension principles with a quoted evidence line; reject any score row that lacks an evidence quote.
rule.rationale: Principle scoring without evidence reduces to opinion; only cited artefacts survive a CCBA/CBAP audit and provide actionable gap data.

list.item: Analyze to Determine What is Valuable

example.note: Scoring "Principle 4: 4/5" with no quoted evidence. Agents routinely produce scores without source anchors, making the audit unverifiable.

rule.statement: Choose one canonical framework as the anchor and label techniques borrowed from others explicitly; do not mix IIBA AE + DA + SAFe vocabulary without declaring the anchor.
rule.rationale: Mixed vocabulary creates false precision and forces future BAs to relitigate framework choices every sprint.

example.note: IIBA Agile Extension: scope single team to 2-3 teams, low adoption cost, principles-based. Disciplined Agile: mid-org mixed cadences, 6 lifecycle options, explicit BA roles. SAFe: large enterprise 50+ people, highest adoption cost, strongest scaling guidance. Do not propose SAFe for teams under 50 people.

antipattern.description: Treating ceremony coverage as principle adherence — "we run stand-ups therefore we Stimulate Collaboration."
antipattern.reason: Ceremonies are necessary but not sufficient; principle adherence requires evidence of behaviour change, not attendance records.

rule.statement: Select a single DA primary lifecycle and at most one alternative; reject "all lifecycles apply" outputs.
rule.rationale: DA documents 6 lifecycles; an agent given all options will select all, making the choice useless for team planning.

list.item: Agile — iterative, team-scoped, low process weight.
Continuous Delivery: Agile — CD pipeline required, assumes mature DevOps.
Continuous Delivery: Lean — CD + Lean, highest DevOps maturity bar.
Exploratory — R&D, proof-of-concept, unknown solution space.
Program — multi-team coordination, large solution scope.

rule.statement: Run the framework fit as a knowledge-retrieval and synthesis pipeline, not a periodic cron; trigger is one-shot on initiative setup or after a major team/scope change.
rule.rationale: Framework choices are not daily tasks; automating them on a schedule produces stale recommendations that erode trust in the analysis.

list.item: Step 1: Ingest team state (constitution, roadmap, sample backlog, retro notes, regulatory profile).
Step 2: Score IIBA AE seven principles (1-5, with one quoted evidence line per score).
Step 3: Run DA lifecycle fit scoring against team context dimensions (size, DevOps maturity, discovery maturity).
Step 4: Emit framework-fit.md: primary framework, secondary alternative, top 3 principle gaps with concrete remediation actions.
Step 5: Hand off operational work (story refinement, AC generation) to the business-analyst variant agent.

rule.statement: Pin framework versions in the constitution; refresh the framework-fit report after major team or scope changes.
rule.rationale: Without version pins, agent output drifts as upstream content updates; without refresh triggers, recommendations become stale after M&A, new regulatory load, or team size doubling.

text.reference: Framework comparison matrix and IIBA seven-principles scorecard skeleton.
